headlines: Morgan Stanley moves closer to a U.S. spot Bitcoin ETF (MSBT) with new S-1 amendment
Circle announces cirBTC — a 1:1 wrapped Bitcoin for institutions
Stablecoin monthly volume tops US ACH for the first time
Bitcoin miner Riot sold 3,778 BTC in Q1 as miners trim reserves
Metaplanet bought 5,075 BTC in Q1 and became a top public Bitcoin treasury holder

articles.title: Morgan Stanley signals near-launch for spot Bitcoin ETF (MSBT)
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley filed an S-1 amendment for the Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Trust (ticker MSBT) that lays out custody, listing and a low 0.14% fee — a clear sign the bank is close to launching a US spot Bitcoin ETF. (Apr 1–3, 2026)

articles.title: Circle unveils cirBTC — a 1:1 wrapped Bitcoin aimed at institutions
articles.summary: Circle announced cirBTC, a Bitcoin-backed token (1:1) that will launch on Ethereum and Circle’s Arc chain with on-chain reserve verification to serve OTC desks, market makers and DeFi. (Apr 3, 2026)

articles.title: Stablecoins processed more monthly volume than the US ACH network
articles.summary: Data shows stablecoin transfers hit roughly $7.2T in February, surpassing the US ACH network’s $6.8T — a major milestone for stablecoins as a payments and trading backbone. (Reported Apr 3, 2026)

articles.title: Riot Platforms sold 3,778 BTC in Q1 amid miner selling pressure
articles.summary: Bitcoin miner Riot disclosed selling 3,778 BTC in Q1 (~$289.5M at average prices) as listed miners continue to trim holdings under market stress. This adds to a wave of miner and corporate sales. (Apr 3, 2026)

articles.title: Metaplanet bought 5,075 BTC in Q1 and joins the largest public Bitcoin treasuries
articles.summary: Tokyo-listed Metaplanet bought 5,075 BTC (~$405M) during Q1 2026, bringing its total holdings to 40,177 BTC and making it one of the largest public corporate Bitcoin treasuries. (Apr 2, 2026)

articles.title: Solana handled a record wave of stablecoin transfers — $650B in February
articles.summary: On-chain reports show Solana processed roughly $650B of stablecoin transactions in February 2026, underscoring the chain’s growth as a high-speed settlement layer for stablecoins and tokenized assets. (Reported early April 2026)

articles.title: Telegram wallet adds perpetual futures trading via Lighter
articles.summary: Telegram’s wallet integration now supports perpetual futures trading (up to 50×) through a Lighter DEX integration, expanding crypto derivatives to Telegram’s large user base. (Apr 2, 2026)

articles.title: CFTC settles with former FTX engineer Nishad Singh for $3.7M
articles.summary: The CFTC finalized a settlement requiring former FTX engineer Nishad Singh to pay $3.7M in disgorgement, closing one of the regulator’s actions tied to the FTX collapse. (Settlement public Apr 1, 2026)
